violations.description: Largest test file is 1900 lines; suite audit found 113 test/support files over the 300 line DoD.
violations.suggestion: Split by behavior slices and add a CI guard for new/changed test files over 300 lines.

violations.description: Actor.StateManager reflection injection appears in 35 sites, while AggregateActorTestHelper already contains one helper.
violations.suggestion: Promote a reusable ActorTestHarness.AttachStateManager helper to Hexalith.EventStore.Testing and migrate all direct reflection sites.

violations.description: Only 2 Priority traits were found across roughly 5288 Fact/Theory declarations.
violations.suggestion: Add P0/P1/P2/P3 traits for smoke and high-risk integration coverage so selective execution is possible.

violations.description: Suite still mixes xUnit Assert.* and Shouldly heavily despite project context preferring Shouldly.
violations.suggestion: Convert opportunistically and add an analyzer or review rule for new tests.

violations.description: Project_IsConfigured asserts true and gives no behavioral signal.
violations.suggestion: Delete it or replace with a real configuration/build invariant.

violations.description: Browser E2E tests rely on CSS selectors for navigation and shell elements.
violations.suggestion: Prefer Playwright .NET role locators or stable test IDs for user-visible navigation contracts.

recommendations: Stop new oversized files with a CI/reporting guard.
Consolidate Actor.StateManager reflection into one reusable harness.
Backfill priority traits on smoke, auth, command lifecycle, and actor/data-isolation tests.
Move E2E selectors toward role/test-id locators.
summary: Maintainability is the weakest dimension. The suite is broad and valuable, but it is accumulating large files and repeated harness code faster than local patterns are being consolidated.
